---
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cran.rstudio.com/"))

# List of required packages
packages <- c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "caret", "randomForest", "knitr", "ggplot2", "readxl")

# Install any missing packages
to_install <- packages[!packages %in% installed.packages()]
if(length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install)

# Load all packages
lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE)

# Load the data
data <- readxl::read_excel("/Users/gyansharma/Documents/MGT 599 Reyes Holdings Student Files/2024_Event.xlsx", sheet = 1)
head(data)
```

```{r data-preprocessing}
data <- data %>%
    mutate(record_date_utc = as_date(record_date_utc),
           year = year(record_date_utc),
           month = month(record_date_utc),
           day = day(record_date_utc),
           hour = hour(record_date_utc))

# Display data structure
str(data)
```

```{r descriptive-analysis}
# Trigger event frequency
trigger_summary <- data %>%
    summarise(across(contains("trigger"), sum)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "event_type", values_to = "count")

ggplot(trigger_summary, aes(x = reorder(event_type, count), y = count)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = "Frequency of Each Trigger Event", x = "Event Type", y = "Count")

# Risk by time of day (hourly analysis)
time_risk <- data %>%
    group_by(hour) %>%
    summarise(total_risk = sum(event_score))

# Plot with data labels
ggplot(time_risk, aes(x = hour, y = total_risk)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1) +
    geom_point(color = "red", size = 2) +
    geom_text(aes(label = total_risk), vjust = -0.5, color = "darkblue") +
    labs(title = "Risk by Hour", x = "Hour of Day", y = "Total Risk Score") +
    theme_minimal()

# Risk by day of the week
day_risk <- data %>%
    mutate(day_of_week = wday(record_date_utc, label = TRUE)) %>%
    group_by(day_of_week) %>%
    summarise(total_risk = sum(event_score))

# Plot with data labels
ggplot(day_risk, aes(x = day_of_week, y = total_risk)) +
    geom_col(fill = "skyblue", color = "darkblue") +
    geom_text(aes(label = total_risk), vjust = -0.5, color = "darkblue") +
    labs(title = "Risk by Day of the Week", x = "Day of the Week", y = "Total Risk Score") +
    theme_minimal()
```

```{r predictive-modeling}
set.seed(123)
# Create training and test sets
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(data$event_score, p = .7, list = FALSE)
train_data <- data[trainIndex, ]
test_data <- data[-trainIndex, ]

# Train random forest model
model <- randomForest(event_score ~ ., data = train_data, importance = TRUE, ntree = 500)
1
# Generate predictions on the test set
predictions <- predict(model, test_data)

# Convert event_score in test_data and predictions to factors with the same levels for confusion matrix
test_data$event_score <- factor(test_data$event_score)
predictions <- factor(predictions, levels = levels(test_data$event_score))

# Evaluate model performance with confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(predictions, test_data$event_score)

# Generate predictions
predictions <- predict(model, test_data)

# Convert importance matrix to a data frame
importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance(model))

# Print the column names to identify the actual importance measure column
print(colnames(importance_df))

# Arrange by '%IncMSE' or 'IncNodePurity' to get the top 10 important variables
importance_df <- importance_df %>%
    rownames_to_column(var = "variable") %>%
    arrange(desc(`%IncMSE`)) %>%  # Using `%IncMSE` for ranking
    head(10)

# Display the table
kable(importance_df)

```

```{r top-variables-plot}
ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(variable, `%IncMSE`), y = `%IncMSE`)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(`%IncMSE`, 1)),  # Display labels with 1 decimal place
              hjust = -0.2,                      # Position labels slightly outside the bar
              size = 3) +                        # Adjust text size as needed
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = "Top 10 Important Variables for Predicting Event Score",
         x = "Variable", y = "Importance (% IncMSE)") +
    theme_minimal() +                            # Use a clean theme
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) # Center-align the title

```

```{r prescriptive-analysis}
cat("### Recommendations for Safety Initiatives:")
cat("1. Implement targeted coaching for drivers on the top 3 high-risk behaviors identified.")
cat("2. Increase monitoring and prevention efforts during peak risk hours as identified in the analysis.")
cat("3. Review operational practices on days with higher risk and consider policy changes.")
cat("4. Prioritize maintenance and vehicle checks on trucks showing higher scores for specific triggers.")
cat("5. Use the data to monetize by offering custom reports to insurers or fleet partners.")
```
